{"id":"project-LICHEN-zg2d","title":"coap_oscore_protect_response uses unbounded stack allocation","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/oscore/coap_oscore.c
Lines: 149-151

The coap_oscore_protect_response function allocates a large buffer on the stack:

```c
uint8_t ciphertext[CONFIG_LICHEN_OSCORE_PLAINTEXT_MAX + OSCORE_TAG_LEN];
size_t ciphertext_len = sizeof(ciphertext);
uint8_t oscore_opt[16];
```

CONFIG_LICHEN_OSCORE_PLAINTEXT_MAX is a configurable value. If set to a large value (e.g., 4096), this consumes 4104 bytes of stack per call.

The same pattern exists in oscore.c for oscore_protect_request (line 1398) and oscore_unprotect_request (line 1610), but those are the core functions that need the buffer.

For coap_oscore_protect_response, the ciphertext buffer could potentially be:
1. Passed in by the caller
2. Allocated from a heap/pool
3. Made static with mutex protection

On constrained devices with limited stack (e.g., 1KB), this could cause stack overflow.

The hkdf.h header at lines 12-14 documents ~500 bytes for key derivation, but this function adds another ~4KB potentially.

Severity: Medium - can cause stack overflow on constrained devices with large CONFIG_LICHEN_OSCORE_PLAINTEXT_MAX.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:51:59.284086Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:51:59.284116Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Commit: 334f2a279\nMoved large ciphertext buffer to static in coap_oscore.c to eliminate stack usage on constrained devices (e.g. STM32WL 64KB). Matches project patterns for no-heap hot paths. oscore_opt/token remain on stack (small)."}

Replaced stack array with static coap_response_ciphertext buffer. Eliminates risk of stack overflow for large CONFIG_LICHEN_OSCORE_PLAINTEXT_MAX on constrained platforms. Follows no-heap pattern from AGENTS.md.
